rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE KING et al VS. AC AND S,INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 10, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof. Plaintiff has failed to present evidence of triable issue of fact because plaintiff has not linked decedent to asbestos containing products of defendant. Defendant's objections are sustained as to exhibit A, exhibit G, exhibit H, exhibit I and Exhibit O; the others are overruled. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE KING et al VS. AC AND S,INC. et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 10, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT PNEUMO ABEX CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof. Plaintiff has failed to present evidence of triable issue of fact because plaintiff has not linked decedent to asbestos containing products of defendant. Defendant's objections 4, 5, 6, 9, 12 and 13 are sustained; the others overruled. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: SURE BUILDING PRODUCTS, INC. et al VS. TCC STEEL, A KOREAN COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 10, 2008, line 7. PLAINTIFFS SURE BUILDING PRODUCTS, INC, DELTA BUILDING PRODUCTS, LTD AND OMEGA ROOF, INC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is denied based on Davis v. Marin (2000) 80 Cal. App. 4th 380, 387. Absent the running of the statute of limitations, the complaining party's knowledge of the defendant is not a relevant issue. See "The Rutter Guide, Civ. Pro. Before Trial," Chapter 6-E, Amended and Supplemental Pleadings section 6.617.10. Plaintiffs have failed to provide a sufficient showing for the Court to dismiss the action as a sham complaint given the testimony of Mr. Testa. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For An Osc Re: Contempt To Be Issued To Deft Michelman & Robinson, Llp And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $4240;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 10, 2008, line 8. PLAINTIFF KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC'S Motion For An O S C Re: Contempt To Be Issued To Defendant Michelman & Robinson, L L P And Request For Sanctions is denied. Failure to demonstrate a knowing violation of a Court Order. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 10, 2008, line 8. PLAINTIFFS RAUL AGUILAR AND AGUILAR LAW OFFICES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. Failure to sustain burden of proof. Under James v. Ahmanson & Co. (1969) 1 Cal. 3d 93, 107, Sebastinelli may state a claim for injury to his personal interests as contrasted with corporate interests. Aguilar's declaration fails to demonstrate that Sebastinelli's individual interests were not adversely affected by the wrongs alleged and Sebastinelli asserts he is suing for personal injuries. This also raises a triable issue of fact on this issue. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: LEE CANTRELL WILLIAMS et al VS. AMFLEX PLASTICS, INCORPORATED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 10, 2008, line 9. DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. A hearing is required. Defendant has shifted the burden of proof. Plaintiff's special interrogatory responses constitute more than objections and as a consequence are factually devoid. Hearing required to address defendant's objections to the Wilson declaration and defendant's alternative request for a continuance. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD E. GRAMMENS VS. NEWMARK PACIFIC, INC., DOING BUSINESS AS NEWMARK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 10, 2008, line 17. CROSS DEFENDANT EDWARD GRAMMENS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There are triable issues of fact as to whether the June 8, 2005 agreement and the termination agreement were procured by fraud or concealment of material facts. Parole evidence does not bar defendant's evidence for the alleged fraud and concealment relates to an issue that was not a part of either contract. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. METRO CAB COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-07-463546, CGC-07-466072 WITH CASE CGC-07-466348
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 10, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANTS METRO CAB COMPANY and LIWEN LI'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-07-463546, CGC-07-466072 WITH CASE CGC-07-466348 is granted. The issue of prejudice may be addressed by the trial judge. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DELOIS VS. BARRETT BLOCK PARTNERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 10, 2008, line 12. DEFENDANTS BARRETT BLOCK PARTNERS AND JOHN BARRETT'S Motion For Attorney's Fees. The motion is granted awarding defendants $10,660.00 in costs and attorneys' fees. The Court has reduced the request by 20% to reflect the fact that the contract causes of action remain. Although defendants' pleadings had a common theme, the papers directed material arguments to have the contract claims dismissed. Given the Court's ruling on those claims, a reduction is warranted. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DEANNA L AUSSELET et al VS. WPX COURIER.LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Change Of Venue; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 10, 2008, line 13. DEFENDANTS W P X COURIER, LLC AND RAPID DELIVERY SOLUTIONS, L L C'S Motion For Change Of Venue is granted based on the convenience of third party witnesses, the fact that the accident occurred in Sutter County and plaintiff's failure to demonstrate through the records of the Secretary of State the principal place of business of the corporate defendant. =(302/PJM/xAM)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. BRIDGET BADASAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 10, 2008, line 18. PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied.
